messages.text: After the FPS matches, Tendou-san brought out a famous action brawler. Four-player free-for-all with stage hazards and powerful items. The kind where a lucky pickup could flip the entire match.
But during the second round, an odd feeling crept in. No one was swapping characters between matches.

messages.name: Keita
messages.text: Wait... none of you are changing characters?

messages.name: Karen
messages.text: I specialize in this one.

messages.name: Tasuku
messages.text: I'm a beginner. I want to master one character at a time.

messages.name: Nina
messages.text: ...I just happen to be worst with this one.
Despite my unease, we kept playing. After six matches, still no one switched. I was the only one picking different fighters every round.

messages.name: Nina
messages.text: Hey. Just stick to one. The one in the corner is strong and easy.

messages.name: Keita
messages.text: Oh! Thanks, senpai. I'll use it... this time.
She gave me a puzzled look. 'This time'?
The matches started feeling monotonous. I was also the only one scrambling for items while everyone else fought straight hand-to-hand. My performance was just... mediocre.
After about ten rounds, Tendou-san checked the clock and called it.

messages.name: Karen
messages.text: We got a little carried away. Alright, that's it! Good work, everyone~

messages.name: Karen
messages.text: So... do you two have any reason for getting into games?
Tendou-san told her story first. An older girl in her neighborhood adored games. After she moved away, Tendou-san played less often, but she still cherishes those memories.

Kase-senpai was drilled from childhood by his mercenary father. Nina-senpai set high goals to save a fighting-game-addicted friend. The two absent members: an RPG princess and a girl chasing a pro gamer who stole her heirloom.

messages.name: Keita
messages.text: What kind of club is this?!

messages.name: Keita
messages.text: I don't have any special reason. I just... like games.

messages.name: Keita
messages.text: Is it wrong to love something without some grand philosophy behind it?

messages.name: Karen
messages.text: No... of course not.

messages.name: Tasuku
messages.text: Since losing my memory, the only thing I can do is that puzzle game.

messages.name: Keita
messages.text: Traitor——!!

messages.name: Karen
messages.text: There's no rule that you must improve. But the club's philosophy is encouraging each other to grow. Watching you two play today... I would love for you both to join us.

messages.name: Tasuku
messages.text: I had a wonderful time. Please let me join.
Tendou-san turned to me, her smile like a dream I could reach out and grasp.

messages.name: Karen
messages.text: What about you, Amano-kun? Won't you join the game club with me?
If I said yes, I could have everything. That dreamlike life. Members who might eventually respect a complete amateur like me.
But that wasn't the real issue. For me, games were never about competition. They were comfort. They were salvation. They were pure entertainment.
I acknowledge that the club is full of amazing people. I respect their way of playing games.
But I'm stubborn about one thing. I want games to stay my sanctuary.
So with resolute clarity, I rejected the offer.

messages.name: Keita
messages.text: No, I'll pass. This club... doesn't seem to have the game I want to play.
